name: committed_length_equals_state_length
reason: `committed()` uses `map` which should preserve length — if it didn't, indexing into `committed()` would be out of bounds

name: can_crash_as_committed_when_no_writes
code:
proof fn phi_2_can_crash_as_committed_when_no_writes(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView)
    requires
        forall|k: int| 0 <= k < view.len() ==> (#[trigger] view.state[k].outstanding_write).is_none(),
    ensures
        view.can_crash_as(view.committed()),
{
}
reason: When no outstanding writes exist, the committed state should be a valid crash state — if `can_crash_as` rejected the committed view, no valid crash state would exist

name: chunk_size_divides_addr_range
code:
proof fn phi_3_chunk_size_divides_addr_range(a: int, b: int)
    requires
        a >= 0,
        b >= 0,
        a / const_persistence_chunk_size() == b / const_persistence_chunk_size(),
        a != b,
    ensures
        (a - b).abs() < const_persistence_chunk_size(),
{
}
reason: Two addresses in the same chunk should be within chunk_size of each other — if the chunk calculation allowed distant addresses in the same chunk, crash atomicity would span too large a range

name: crash_state_allows_mixed_chunks
reason: Different chunks can independently choose between flushed and committed states — one chunk crashes as committed while another crashes as flushed; if the crash model forced all-or-nothing across chunks, it would be too strong

name: flush_byte_with_outstanding_ignores_old
code:
proof fn phi_5_flush_byte_with_outstanding_ignores_old(old_val: u8, new_val: u8)
    requires
        old_val != new_val,
    ensures
        (PersistentMemoryByte { state_at_last_flush: old_val, outstanding_write: Some(new_val) }).flush_byte() == new_val,
{
}
reason: `flush_byte` with an outstanding write should return the new value, not the old — if it returned the old value, flushing would be a no-op and writes would never persist
